title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create virtual environment and install all required packages for point cloud processing, flow matching, and model training.

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.1+ with CUDA: pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step3: Install supporting libraries: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn matplotlib seaborn tqdm einops h5py
steps.step4: Verify CUDA available: python -c 'import torch; assert torch.cuda.is_available(); print(f"GPU: {torch.cuda.get_device_name(0)}")'

title: Download and Preprocess KITTI-360 Subset
description: Download KITTI-360 LiDAR data subset and create preprocessed tensors. Using 5K train, 1K val for 8-hour budget.

steps.step1: Download KITTI-360 Velodyne: Fetch sequences 00-03 (train) and 09 (val) using official scripts
steps.step3: Extract point clouds: Load .bin files (N,4), keep x,y,z coordinates
steps.step4: Filter and downsample: Remove ground (z > -1.6m), subsample to N=8192 points per scan
steps.step5: Compute radial distances: r = sqrt(x^2 + y^2), store normalized r/R_max where R_max=80m
steps.step6: Create splits: Train=5000 scans from sequences 00-03, Val=1000 scans from sequence 09
steps.step7: Save tensors: Store as .pt files with keys 'points' (8192,3), 'radial_dist' (8192,)
steps.step8: Verify: Check distance distribution - expect ~65% near (0-20m), ~25% mid (20-50m), ~10% far (50m+)

title: Baseline 1: Standard Flow Matching (Uniform Weighting)
description: Train standard flow matching with uniform MSE loss. Simple baseline representing current practice.

steps.step1: Implement PointTransformer: 4 layers, 256 hidden dim, 4 heads, ~4M parameters in src/models.py
steps.step2: Implement velocity network: Input x_t (B,N,3) and timestep t, output velocity v (B,N,3)
steps.step3: Implement uniform FM loss: L = mean(||v_theta(x_t, t) - (x_1 - x_0)||^2) equally weighted for all points
steps.step4: Set hyperparameters: Batch size 32, LR 2e-4 with cosine decay, AdamW optimizer, weight decay 1e-4
steps.step5: Train: 70 epochs with 2 random seeds (42, 123), save best checkpoint by validation loss
steps.step6: Generate: 50-step Euler sampling, create 200 samples for evaluation
steps.step7: Evaluate: Compute CD-overall, CD-near (0-20m), CD-mid (20-50m), CD-far (50m+), 1-NN accuracy

title: Baseline 2: Density-Weighted Flow Matching
description: Stronger baseline using local point density weighting. Tests if any non-uniform weighting helps.

steps.step1: Compute local density: For each point, find k=8 nearest neighbors, density_i = 1/(mean(knn_dists) + 1e-6)
steps.step2: Normalize weights: w_i = density_i / mean(density), clip to [0.5, 2.0] for stability
steps.step3: Modify loss: L = mean(w_i * ||v_theta(x_t, t) - (x_1 - x_0)||^2)
steps.step4: Use identical architecture and hyperparameters as Baseline 1 for fair comparison
steps.step6: Generate samples and evaluate with same protocol

title: DistFlow-IDW: Inverse Distance Weighting
description: Main proposed method with hand-designed inverse distance weighting. Primary contribution.

steps.step1: Implement IDW weighting: w(r) = 1 + 1.0 * (r/80.0)^2.0, clip to [1.0, 3.0]
steps.step2: Implement DistFlow loss: L = mean(w(r_i) * ||v_theta(x_t, t, dist_stats) - (x_1 - x_0)||^2)
steps.step3: Implement distance-conditioned FiLM: Compute mean_r, max_r of input, concatenate to timestep embedding
steps.step4: Use PointTransformer with same size as baselines (~4M params)
steps.step5: Set hyperparameters: Batch 32, LR 2e-4, alpha=1.0, beta=2.0, R_max=80m
steps.step6: Train 70 epochs with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) - 3 seeds for statistical validity of main claim
steps.step8: Evaluate all metrics including distance-stratified CD

title: DistFlow-LAW: Learned Adaptive Weighting
description: Alternative with learned MLP weighting (4->32->16->1, ~1K params) to compare against IDW.

steps.step1: Implement weight MLP: Input [mean_knn, std_knn, r/R_max, z/5], output weight via sigmoid to [0.5, 2.0]
steps.step2: Compute features: k=8 nearest neighbor statistics per point
steps.step3: Train MLP end-to-end with flow objective
steps.step4: Use same backbone, FiLM, and hyperparameters as IDW
steps.step7: Compare LAW vs IDW: determine if learned beats hand-designed

title: Ablation: Without Distance-Conditioned FiLM
description: Remove FiLM conditioning to isolate its contribution. Keep IDW loss but remove distance statistics from timestep.

steps.step1: Modify architecture: Remove distance statistics, use only sinusoidal t embedding
steps.step2: Keep IDW loss weighting with alpha=1.0, beta=2.0
steps.step3: Use same hyperparameters as main IDW experiment
steps.step5: Evaluate and compute difference from full DistFlow-IDW

title: Ablation: Without Multi-Scale Stratification
description: Test uniform sampling vs stratified sampling. Use uniform point sampling instead of distance-aware buckets.

steps.step1: Implement uniform sampling: Randomly sample 8192 points from full scan (no distance buckets)
steps.step2: Keep IDW loss and FiLM conditioning
steps.step5: Evaluate and compare to full method

title: Sensitivity: Beta Parameter Study
description: Test beta values (1.0, 1.5, 2.5) for distance weighting curvature. Single seed, 50 epochs each.

steps.step4: Use seed 42, 40 epochs each (sufficient for sensitivity), same other hyperparameters

title: Statistical Testing and Final Evaluation
description: Perform rigorous statistical tests to validate hypothesis. Check significance of CD-far improvement.

steps.step1: Load all experimental results from JSON files
steps.step2: Compute mean and std across seeds for each method
steps.step3: Paired t-test: Compare DistFlow-IDW (3 seeds) vs Baseline Uniform (2 seeds) on CD-far, report p-value
steps.step4: Check non-degradation: Test CD-near difference not significant (p > 0.05)
steps.step6: Create comparison table: Method | CD-overall | CD-near | CD-mid | CD-far | 1-NN
steps.step7: Check success criteria: CD-far improvement >= 25% and p < 0.05

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create all visualizations for the research paper including sample comparisons, metric plots, and ablation analysis.

steps.step1: Generate point cloud visualizations: Side-by-side of Baseline, DistFlow, Ground Truth (bird's eye view)
steps.step2: Color by distance: Blue=near, green=mid, red=far to visualize far-field improvements
steps.step3: Create metric bar chart: Compare CD metrics across methods with error bars from seeds
steps.step4: Create ablation plot: Show contribution of FiLM and Stratification components
steps.step5: Plot sensitivity curve: CD-far vs beta parameter
steps.step6: Generate qualitative examples: 5 scenes showing distant structure improvements
steps.step7: Save figures to outputs/visualizations/ as high-res PNG
